name: text-lloom

metadata.name: text_lloom
metadata.summary: Concept Induction to analyze unstructured text

documentation: Directory for the LLooM python package

modules.name: text_lloom.llm
documentation:
llm.py
------
This file contains utility functions for processing calls to LLMs.

functions.name: text_lloom.llm.base_api_wrapper
documentation: Wrapper for calling the base OpenAI API

functions.name: text_lloom.llm.multi_query_gpt
documentation: Internal function making calls to LLM; runs a single LLM query

functions.name: text_lloom.llm.multi_query_gpt_wrapper
documentation: Run multiple LLM queries

methods.name: text_lloom.llm.Model.__init__
documentation: Specification to run LLooM operator with a specific large language model
- name: str, name of the model (ex: "gpt-3.5-turbo")
- setup_fn: function, function to set up the LLM client
- fn: function, function to call the model (i.e., to run LLM prompt)
- api_key: str (optional), API key
- rate_limit: tuple (optional), (n_requests, wait_time_secs)

methods.name: text_lloom.llm.EmbedModel.__init__
documentation: Specification to run LLooM operator with a specific embedding model
- name: str, name of the model (ex: "text-embedding-ada-002")
- setup_fn: function, function to set up the embedding client
- fn: function, function to call the model (i.e., to fetch embedding)
- api_key: str (optional), API key
- batch_size: int (optional), maximum batch size for embeddings (None for no batching)

methods.name: text_lloom.llm.OpenAIModel.__init__
documentation: OpenAIModel class for OpenAI LLMs
Adds the following parameters for token and cost tracking:
- context_window: int (optional), total tokens shared between input and output
- cost: float (optional), cost per token (input_cost, output_cost)

methods.name: text_lloom.llm.OpenAIEmbedModel.__init__
documentation: OpenAIEmbedModel class for OpenAI embedding models
Adds the following parameters for cost tracking:
- cost: float (optional), cost per token (input_cost, output_cost)

variables.name: text_lloom._DEV
documentation: switch to False for production

modules.name: text_lloom.llm_openai
documentation: OpenAI custom functions

functions.name: text_lloom.llm_openai.setup_llm_fn
documentation: SETUP functions

functions.name: text_lloom.llm_openai.call_llm_fn
documentation: MODEL CALL functions

documentation: TOKEN + COST functions

modules.name: text_lloom.concept
documentation: Concept induction concept functions
Imports

modules.name: text_lloom.workbench
documentation: Concept induction session functions
Imports

methods.name: text_lloom.workbench.lloom.preprocess_df
documentation: Preprocesses input dataframe

methods.name: text_lloom.workbench.lloom.bold_txt
documentation: Printed text formatting

methods.name: text_lloom.workbench.lloom.estimate_gen_cost
documentation: Estimate cost of generation for the given params

methods.name: text_lloom.workbench.lloom.estimate_score_cost
documentation: Estimate cost of scoring for the given number of concepts

methods.name: text_lloom.workbench.lloom.score
documentation: Score the specified concepts
Only score the concepts that are active

methods.name: text_lloom.workbench.lloom.vis
documentation: Visualize concept induction results
Parameters:
- cols_to_show: list (additional column names to show in the tables)
- slice_col: str (column name with which to slice data)
- max_slice_bins: int (Optional: for numeric columns, the maximum number of bins to create)
- slice_bounds: list (Optional: for numeric columns, manual bin boundaries to use)
- show_highlights: bool (whether to show text highlights)
- norm_by: str (how to normalize scores: "concept" or "slice")
- export_df: bool (whether to return a dataframe for export)

modules.name: text_lloom.concept_induction
documentation: Main concept induction library functions
Imports

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.distill_filter
documentation: Input:
- text_df: DataFrame (columns: doc_id, doc)
Parameters: model_name, n_quotes, seed
Output: quote_df (columns: doc_id, quote)

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.distill_summarize
documentation: Input: text_df (columns: doc_id, doc)
--> text could be original or filtered (quotes)
Parameters: n_bullets, n_words_per_bullet, seed
Output: bullet_df (columns: doc_id, bullet)

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.cluster
documentation: Input: text_df (columns: doc_id, doc)
--> text could be original, filtered (quotes), and/or summarized (bullets)
Parameters: n_clusters
Output: cluster_df (columns: doc_id, doc, cluster_id)

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.synthesize
documentation: Input: cluster_df (columns: doc_id, doc, cluster_id)
Parameters: n_concepts
Output:
- concepts: dict (concept_id -> concept dict)
- concept_df: DataFrame (columns: doc_id, doc, concept_id, concept_name, concept_prompt)

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.review
documentation: Input: concept_df (columns: doc_id, text, concept_id, concept_name, concept_prompt)
Parameters: n_concepts
Output:
- concepts: dict (concept_id -> Concept)
- concept_df: DataFrame (columns: doc_id, text, concept_id, concept_name, concept_prompt)

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.review_remove
documentation: Model removes concepts that are too specific or too general
Input: concept_df (columns: doc_id, text, concept_id, concept_name, concept_prompt)
Parameters: n_concepts
Output:
- concepts: dict (concept_id -> Concept)
- concept_df: DataFrame (columns: doc_id, text, concept_id, concept_name, concept_prompt)

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.review_merge
documentation: Model merges concepts that are similar or overlapping
Input: concept_df (columns: doc_id, text, concept_id, concept_name, concept_prompt)
Parameters: n_concepts
Output:
- concepts: dict (concept_id -> Concept)
- concept_df: DataFrame (columns: doc_id, text, concept_id, concept_name, concept_prompt)

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.review_select
documentation: Model selects the best concepts up to `max_concepts`
Input: concepts (concept_id -> Concept)
Parameters: max_concepts, model
Output: selected_concepts: dict (concept_id -> Concept)

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.score_helper
documentation: Performs scoring for one concept

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.score_concepts
documentation: Performs scoring for all concepts
Input: concepts, text_df (columns: doc_id, text)
--> text could be original, filtered (quotes), and/or summarized (bullets)
Parameters: threshold
Output: score_df (columns: doc_id, text, concept_id, concept_name, concept_prompt, score, highlight)

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.refine
documentation: Based on concept scoring, refine concepts
Input: score_df (columns: doc_id, text, concept_id, concept_name, concept_prompt, score, highlight), concepts
Parameters:
- threshold (float): minimum score of positive class
- generic_threshold (float): min fraction concept matches to be considered generic
- rare_threshold (float): max fraction of concept matches considered rare
Output:
- concepts (dict)

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.get_not_covered
documentation: Returns ids of not-covered documents

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.get_covered_by_generic
documentation: Returns ids of covered-by-generic documents

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.loop
documentation: Input: score_df (columns: doc_id, doc, concept_id, score)
Output: text_df (columns: doc_id, doc) --> filtered set of documents to run further iterations of concept induction
Returns None if (1) there are zero remaining documents after filtering or (2) there are no changes in the number of documents after filtering

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.auto_eval
documentation: Eval helpers
Input: items (dict), concepts (list of strings)
Output: concepts_found (dict: concept_id -> list of items), concept_coverage (float)

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.format_scores
documentation: Adds color-background styling for score columns to match score value

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.format_bullets
documentation: Converts a list of bullet strings to a formatted unordered list

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.format_highlight
documentation: Adds color-background styling for highlight columns to match score value

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.clean_score
documentation: Transforms scores to be between 0 and 1

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.is_outlier
documentation: Check if the given example (represented by a row) is an outlier (i.e., doesn't match any of the concepts)

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.get_concept_col_df
documentation: Helper function for `prep_vis_dfs()` to generate a dataframe with a column containing the scores for each concept
The output dataframe has: doc_id_col, doc_col, any specified cols_to_show, and a column for each concept (where the column name is the concept name)

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction._slice_fn_cat
documentation: Template slice function for string (categorical) columns

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction._slice_fn_num
documentation: Template slice function for numeric columns

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.slice_bounds_to_bins
documentation: Convert manual slice bounds to bins

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.get_groupings
documentation: Automatically create slice groupings based on slice column
- slice_col: str (column name with which to slice data)
- max_slice_bins: int (Optional: for numeric columns, the maximum number of bins to create)
- slice_bounds: list (Optional: for numeric columns, manual bin boundaries to use)

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.prep_vis_dfs
documentation: Helper function for `visualize()` to generate the underlying dataframes
Parameters:
- threshold: float (minimum score of positive class)

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.visualize
documentation: Generates the in-notebook visualization for concept induction results
Input:
- in_df: DataFrame (includes columns: doc_id_col, doc_col)
- score_df: DataFrame (columns: doc_id_col, text, concept_id, concept_name, concept_prompt, score, highlight)
- doc_col: string (column name for full document text)
- doc_id_col: string (column name for document ID)
- score_col: string (column name for concept score)
- df_filtered: DataFrame (columns: doc_id_col, text_col)
- df_bullets: DataFrame (columns: doc_id_col, text_col)
- concepts: dict (concept_id -> Concept object)
Parameters:
- cols_to_show: list of strings (column names to show in visualization)
- slice_col: string (column name to slice by)
- max_slice_bins: int (maximum number of slices to show)
- slice_bounds: list of numbers (manual boundaries for slices)
- show_highlights: boolean (whether to show highlights)
- norm_by: string (column name to normalize by; either "slice" or "concept")
- debug: boolean (whether to print debug statements)

functions.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.edit_concept
documentation: Edits an existing concept with the specified ID
Input:
- concepts: dict (concept_id -> concept dict) with existing set of concepts
- concept_id: string (ID of the concept to edit)
Parameters:
- new_name: string (Optional, new name of the concept)
- new_prompt: string (Optional, new prompt for the concept)
- new_ex_ids: list of strings (Optional, IDs of examples that match the new concept)
Output:
- concepts: dict (concept_id -> concept dict) with the concept edited

variables.name: text_lloom.concept_induction.NAN_SCORE
documentation: Numerical score to use in place of NaN values for matrix viz
